description_long: This object is a stylized potted coniferous plant, resembling a small fir or pine tree with dense, overlapping, dark green foliage. The leaves are broad, flat, and arranged in layered tiers, giving the plant a full and bushy appearance. The foliage is made up of simple, geometric shapes, suggesting a low-poly or minimalist design. There is no visible trunk or pot, as the leaves extend all the way to the base, obscuring any container. The plant's overall form is conical, tapering smoothly from a wide base to a pointed top. The coloration is uniformly dark green, with subtle shading to indicate depth and layering.
description: A stylized, conical potted conifer with dense, dark green, layered foliage and no visible pot or trunk. The plant has a minimalist, geometric appearance.
description_view_0: The plant appears full and bushy, with layered, flat leaves extending outward and downward, forming a wide base.
description_view_1: This view shows the plant's conical shape most symmetrically, with the foliage appearing evenly distributed on both sides.
description_view_2: The foliage appears slightly denser on the left, with the leaf layers overlapping more closely and the tip leaning subtly to the right.
description_view_3: The plant's conical form is again prominent, with the leaf layers creating a visually balanced silhouette.
